title: rubus subinopertus
intro: Rubus subinopertus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry, is a species of bramble native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. The plant's leaves, stems, and roots have been traditionally used in medicine, exhibiting antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. It is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and alleviating pain. Rubus subinopertus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional Asian cooking.
